description: Add a bouncing dots Loader animation to the plugin card header (and browser tools card header) that activates whenever any tool within that plugin is being invoked, aligned horizontally with the individual tool row loaders

userStories.title: Add Loader animation to PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard headers when any tool is active
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a bouncing dots animation on the plugin card header whenever any tool in that plugin is being invoked, so I can tell at a glance which plugin is working without expanding the card
acceptanceCriteria: When any tool in a plugin has an active invocation (activeTools contains a key matching that plugin), the plugin card header shows a Loader with the same props as the ToolRow Loader (size='sm', count={2}, duration={0.4}, delayStep={80})
When no tools in the plugin are active, no Loader is shown in the header

The Loader in the plugin card header is horizontally aligned with the Loader in ToolRow — both sit immediately to the left of their respective Switch components, inside a 'flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2' wrapper
The same behavior applies to BrowserToolsCard for browser tool invocations (activeTools keys prefixed with 'browser:')

The Loader appears between the PluginMenu/BrowserToolsMenu and the Switch, inside the same px-3 wrapper div that contains the Switch, so that the dots and the switch share the same right-aligned container and the dots visually align with the ToolRow dots above the switch
userStories.notes:
This is a UI-only change in two files: `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx` and `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx`. ## Deriving 'has any active tool' per plugin In `PluginCard.tsx`, compute a boolean from the `activeTools` prop: ```ts const hasActiveTool = pluginTools.some(t => activeTools.has(`${plugin.name}:${t.name}`)); ``` In `BrowserToolsCard.tsx`: ```ts const hasActiveTool = tools.some(t => activeTools.has(`browser:${t.name}`)); ``` ## Where to place the Loader The goal is horizontal alignment with the ToolRow loader. In ToolRow (line 39-47), the Loader sits inside a `
` immediately before the Switch. The PluginCard header Switch is in a similar wrapper at lines 147-159 with `className="flex shrink-0 items-center px-3"`. The Loader should go inside this same wrapper div, right before the ``, and the wrapper needs `gap-2` added to its className so the spacing between the Loader and Switch matches the ToolRow. ### PluginCard change (lines 147-159) Change the Switch wrapper div at line 148 from: ```tsx
e.stopPropagation()} ... role="presentation">
``` to: ```tsx
e.stopPropagation()} ... role="presentation"> {hasActiveTool && }
``` Add `import { Loader } from './retro/Loader.js';` at the top of PluginCard.tsx. ### BrowserToolsCard change (lines 134-142) Same pattern — change the Switch wrapper div at line 135 from: ```tsx
``` to: ```tsx
{hasActiveTool && }
``` Add `import { Loader } from './retro/Loader.js';` at the top of BrowserToolsCard.tsx. This placement ensures the card-level dots are in the exact same horizontal position as the tool-level dots — both are inside a `flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2` wrapper, immediately left of their Switch, at the right edge of the row with `px-3` padding.
